items.question: What does histogram-based split finding do?
options: Bins numeric features and evaluates splits at bin boundaries
Trains neural nets on binned images
items.answer: Bins numeric features and evaluates splits at bin boundaries
items.explanation: Gradient stats are aggregated per bin; split search runs over O(bins) candidates instead of every unique value.

items.question: Which library is most associated with leaf-wise (best-first) tree growth by default?
items.explanation: LightGBM splits the highest-gain leaf each step, controlled by num_leaves.

items.question: What is the main purpose of early_stopping_rounds?
options: Stop training when validation metric stops improving
items.answer: Stop training when validation metric stops improving
items.explanation: Prevents adding trees that overfit after the validation optimum.

items.question: How do XGBoost and LightGBM typically handle NaN at predict time?
options: Follow a learned default split direction
items.answer: Follow a learned default split direction
items.explanation: Each split learns which branch missing values take — distinct from upfront imputation.

items.question: num_leaves in LightGBM is most analogous to which complexity control?
options: Tree leaf count cap under leaf-wise growth
items.answer: Tree leaf count cap under leaf-wise growth
items.explanation: num_leaves limits tree complexity; it is not the same as max_depth in level-wise trees.
